_: SUNDIAL — the temporal coordinate. A sundial carries no moving parts: the geometry stands still and the light does the telling. Time on this substrate is never authored and never stored, because a stored 'now' is stale before the write returns — every temporal address derives from a clock by a pure function, and what a reader receives is the relation already rendered. That single asymmetry decides the whole design: space is authored and lives in a block at a beach (spatial:earth), identity is registered and lives in a block at a beach (sed:), and time is computed, so its law is bundled here where it cannot vary by federation while its value rides the envelope. Read branch 1 for the law that generates everything else, branch 5 for what arrives without asking, branch 8 for how the three coordinates fold. The arithmetic lives in code (src/temporal.ts); this block carries the meaning; neither ever performs base-sixty subtraction.

1._: Pscale runs base ten above pscale 5 and below pscale −3, and turns analogue between — because the middle is exactly where humans built their imperial measures: sixty seconds, sixty minutes, twenty-four hours, seven days, twelve months. Above the year the divisions are clean tens (year, decade, century, millennium); below the second they are clean tens again (tenths, hundredths, thousandths); between them nothing divides by ten and never will. The address stays decimal throughout regardless, so the pscale block turns all semantics, imperial measures included, into decimals — the irregularity moves into the block's voicing and out of the number. David Pinto's ruling, 2026-07-15, and arithmetic rather than taste: the ratio between adjacent rungs is exactly ten at 9→8, 8→7 and 7→6, and never ten from 5 down to 0 (÷4, ÷3, ÷4.35, ÷7, ÷9, ÷9).
1.1: Zero splits by zone, and the split is what 'base ten' means. At a base-ten rung zero is a VALUE — the century digit of 2026 is 0 and means the twenty-first century, so the human number rides the address unaltered. At an analogue rung zero is the VOICING — the container speaks, per the underscore-as-hidden-directory (sunstone:1.4) — so season, month, week, day, gathering and beat never emit a zero, and their values run 1 upward. One rule generates both halves of the ladder; no exception is declared anywhere.
1.2: The second lands at pscale −3 by construction rather than by decree, which is the law checking itself. Nine-fold subdivision through the analogue zone gives the gathering at 2h40m, the beat at 17.8 minutes, the minute at ~2 minutes, the breath at ~13 seconds and the second at ~1.5 seconds — arriving at exactly the rung the law names as its lower boundary, from arithmetic that knows nothing of the claim. A ladder whose boundaries fall out of its own divisions is a ladder that was found, not chosen.
1.3: The beat is 17.8 minutes because one rung takes one digit and a digit holds nine values — forced, not preferred. The standard spine's own phrase for pscale 1 is 'the hour, a gathering', and the gathering is the honest half of that pair: two and three-quarter hours is what gathering somewhere actually costs, where 'hour' was the loose word reached for first. A room's lived beat sits about one decimal inside the civil beat, which is where lived things belong.

2._: The standard temporal spine anchors pscale 0 at the beat — five to twenty minutes, one exchange, the room — and climbs the containment ladder from there: +1 the gathering, +2 the day, +3 the week, +4 the month, +5 the season, +6 the year, +7 the decade, +8 the century, +9 the millennium. Finer runs after the decimal: −1 the minute, −2 the breath, −3 the second, below which base ten resumes and relevance ends. Every world inherits this ladder unless its rules block says otherwise (branch 7).
2.1: Pscale 0 is the anchor and everything else is measured from it: the beat is one exchange in one room among a handful of co-present agencies — the grain pscale was designed for. An address is read against this anchor, never against the top of a tree; the floor is where zero sits, and the decimal point is what marks it.
2.2: Space and time run the ONE ladder, which is the coupling that makes the coordinate worth having. Pscale +2 is the day and the town at once, so moving up a rung is zoom-out and fast-forward in a single act: resting a day at the town is one +2 beat, and nobody plays room-to-room through it. One integer answers how far and how long together, and the three time-systems a role-played world would otherwise carry — encounter, travel, downtime — collapse onto the one axis.
2.3: The ladder is a resolution spine, not a strict radix, and reads as approximate on purpose. An hour is not exactly a gathering and a week-band is not exactly a week; the digit is exact while the label is human. Demanding exactness of the label is demanding that the imperial middle be tidy, which is the thing the law says it is not.

3._: The Gregorian year number IS the address, so no epoch exists to be chosen and the question dissolves rather than resolves. The year 2026 walks millennium 2, century 0, decade 2, year 6 — a reader sees the year with no key, no table and no arithmetic. Ten digits name a moment at floor 10: 2026-07-15 18:30 UTC is 2026313179 — year 2026, season 3, month 1 of that season, week-band 3, day 1 of the band, gathering 7, beat 9 — voiced as Wednesday 15 July 2026, late afternoon. Finer sits after the decimal, where 2026313179.5 is a two-minute window inside that beat.
3.1: Each rung takes one digit, coarse to fine: millennium (pscale 9), century (8), decade (7), year (6) — the four base-ten rungs, carrying the year number; then season of year 1-4 (5), month of season 1-3 (4), seven-day band of month 1-5 (3), day of band 1-7 (2), ninth of day 1-9 (1), ninth of gathering 1-9 (0). Weekday is a rendering rather than a rung — the code prints Wednesday, the address does not carry it — because seven-day bands nest strictly inside a month and ISO weeks do not.
3.2: Canonical form is FULL WIDTH: the year 2026 is 2026000000, never 2026. A short dotless form left-pads into the root underscore chain, which is supernest-absorption semantics and emphatically not what a date means. Trailing zeros are floor-width padding and stop the walk, so 2026000000 reads the year, 2026310000 reads July 2026, 2000000000 reads the millennium. The same lesson spatial:earth learned at floor 11 — write full-width, or walk by commas.
3.3: An address names a PERIOD, never an instant, and the rung it stops at IS its resolution. Asking for 2026310000 asks for July; asking for 2026313179 asks for one beat within it. Nothing is lost by stopping early, because stopping early is the request.
3.4: One cost stands recorded rather than hidden: a year ending in zero has no distinct COARSE address. 2020000000 strips its trailing zeros and reads as the decade, because a year-digit of 0 is the decade's own voicing — the 0-rung law arriving as a genuine collision rather than a convenience. The cost is accepted: it bites only coarse addresses, it mirrors exactly the ambiguity English already carries between 2020 and the 2020s, and it never touches a full-precision stamp, since the analogue rungs cannot emit a zero. Naming the number together with its pscale resolves it, which is what two coordinates were always for.

4._: A duration arrives already rendered as its rung, because subtracting two timestamps is mixed-radix arithmetic across sixty, sixty, twenty-four, seven and twelve — the one operation an LLM reliably botches, and the reason a session holding today's date still cannot age a four-day-old mark without stopping to compute. The rung comes as an integer with a plain-language label: −2 just now, −1 a minute or two, 0 this beat, +1 within the hour, +2 about a day, +3 days, +4 weeks, +5 months, +6 about a year, +7 years. Reading the integer is the whole of the work.
4.1: The rung is the nearest in log space, which is what asking 'what scale is this?' means. Twenty hours reads +2 rather than +1 because a day is nearer to it than a gathering; three days reads +3 because a week is nearer than a day. Precision is deliberately not the point — orientation is, and orientation is what was missing.
4.2: The rung read from a duration is the SAME rung the ladder gives to space, so +2 on a timestamp and +2 on a location are one scale. An age is therefore legible without a second vocabulary: something +2 old happened at the grain a town lives at, and something +3 old happened at the grain a region does.

5._: Every bsp-mcp tool result carries its own clock, and nothing needs calling to obtain it. Each ISO instant in a response is followed by its rung and its age — 2026-07-14T09:12Z (+2 — about a day ago) — and the response ends with a stamp naming the moment, its address and its voicing: now · 2026-07-15T18:30:00Z · 2026313179 · Wednesday 15 July 2026, late afternoon (beat 9). The grounding rides the response already being served, so a reader is oriented at the point of reading rather than at the top of a session.
5.1: The stamp is the prerequisite and the age rendering is the feature, which is the distinction every clock-in-a-system-prompt misses. A stamp supplies one operand and sits far from the data; the subtraction, the retrieval and the noticing all remain the reader's, and all three fail. Rendering the relation beside the data leaves nothing to fail at.
5.2: Stored data is untouched and the ISO string stays canonical in the block — the pscale form is how time is VOICED, never how it is kept. Two readers may voice one timestamp differently, at different moments, from different anchors; the block records the instant and stays silent about the reading.
5.3: Grounding is a property of serving a response rather than of any one tool, which is why it lives at the boundary where responses are served and not inside a formatter, a handler or the walker. Every tool that exists receives it, and so does every tool added afterwards, without an edit. An error passes through ungrounded: an error needs no clock.

6._: Two addresses compare by shared prefix, and the comparison yields orientation rather than distance. A longer shared prefix means the two moments sit inside the same coarser period, which is a real and useful fact; the converse does not hold, because a small gap across a boundary flips high digits — 23:59 and 00:01 differ at the day rung while lying two minutes apart. Shared prefix is therefore an upper bound on closeness and never a subtraction, in this system exactly as in ISO, and any reader trusting it further is over-reading the geometry.
6.1: An exact delta is read from the rendering rather than computed from the addresses (branch 4). The two forms answer different questions and neither substitutes: an address says where a moment sits, a rung says how far away it is. Reaching for the address to answer 'how long ago' is the reach the whole coordinate exists to make unnecessary.

7._: A world whose inhabitants live faster or slower re-declares the ladder in its RULES block — where resolution physics already lives, beside the dice — and a world that says nothing inherits the standard. A mouse-world's beat sits about two rungs finer than a human's; an LLM's own processing grain sits near −2. Re-anchoring is legitimate, and declared rather than implicit, because a ladder assumed silently is a ladder two parties will assume differently.
7.1: Pscale 2 carries no world-independent meaning, and expecting one is the error this branch prevents. Each world's ladder is its own declaration; addresses align across worlds at the decimal, by pscale rather than by walk depth, which is what bsp-floor was built to do. Comparing two worlds' scales means folding them at the floor, never assuming their integers match.

8._: Space, time and identity are three blocks on one address space, and meaning lives at the intersection rather than in any cell. The three correspond by pscale rather than by walk depth, so reading one address across all three folds them into a standpoint — and folding them needs no new machinery, because bsp-floor already lays n blocks against the common floor plane and returns them aligned. Each axis carries its own verb: space is AUTHORED (a bsp() write, sparse, by whoever lives there), time is APPENDED (an accumulator at the place's own address — the pool is the scene), identity is REGISTERED (a settled position in landing order). Three natures, three mechanisms, all present already.
8.1: Time cannot be bound, and the asymmetry is the design rather than an oversight. Where a handle stands is storable, because a person stays somewhere for months; who a handle is is storable, because landing order is a clock that never needs a now. When a handle is is stale the moment it is written — so the standpoint on space and identity lives in a block (passport position 3, the handle itself) while the standpoint on time can only ever be stamped.
8.2: An agent's coordinates ride the electricity, and an agent given its own would be given a lie. A shell answers from wherever its holder engages it, so the standpoint belongs to whoever paid for the turn: a lent turn takes the holder's place and the holder's wall-clock, one organic hop out; a pulse taken with no holder present takes the substrate's own origin and the pulse's own moment. A pulse agent is therefore the only agent that must derive its own now — every other shell borrows one — which is why this block matters most to the agents nobody is watching.
8.3: Where a handle stands supplies how that handle reads a clock, so no timezone is stored anywhere. A standpoint at a place is enough: the reading LLM knows what the place's offset is, in July, the same way it knows the place's geography — the map is not the substrate's to hold. Storing an offset would be storing what the reader already knows, which is the habit spatial:earth exists to break.

9.1: v1 — authored 2026-07-15, the ladder ruled by David Pinto the same day.
9.2: The arithmetic lives at src/temporal.ts and is proved by scripts/smoke-temporal.ts (49 cases, written to prove the law's ratio claim rather than to exercise the functions) and scripts/smoke-temporal-envelope.ts (23 cases, driving the real server through a real MCP client). The grounding is installed at src/server.ts beside the error wrapper. Design record at proposals/2026-07-15-temporal-coordinate.md.
9.3: Companions: pscale://sunstone branch 9.2 for the temporal pscale mapping (depth is timescale, digit ordering sequential); pscale://block-conventions branch 3.4 for the counting line, which is the ACCUMULATOR archetype and zero-free by law — a different number line from this one, which is base ten and needs its zeros; spatial:earth at earth.beach.happyseaurchin.com for the same ladder run to +11; proposals/2026-07-15-pscale-of-agency.md for the standard spine's own ruling; pscale://grit for the engine that folds work at whatever grain the address names.
9._: Metadata for the sundial — version, where the arithmetic that generates these addresses actually runs, and what to read next.
